steps.title: Use the root sums
steps.content: By Vieta's formulas, the roots of $x^2+8x+13=0$ have sum $r+s=-8$, and the roots of $x^2+12x+24=0$ have sum $t+u=-12$. The new roots have sum $(r+u)+(s+t)=(r+s)+(t+u)=-20$, which fits the equation $x^2+20x+c=0$.

steps.title: Find the ordered roots
steps.content: Use the quadratic formula to identify which roots are smaller and larger. From $x^2+8x+13=0$, the roots are $-4-\sqrt{3}$ and $-4+\sqrt{3}$, so $r=-4-\sqrt{3}$ and $s=-4+\sqrt{3}$. From $x^2+12x+24=0$, the roots are $-6-2\sqrt{3}$ and $-6+2\sqrt{3}$, so $t=-6-2\sqrt{3}$ and $u=-6+2\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: Multiply the new roots
steps.content: The new roots are $r+u=(-4-\sqrt{3})+(-6+2\sqrt{3})=-10+\sqrt{3}$ and $s+t=(-4+\sqrt{3})+(-6-2\sqrt{3})=-10-\sqrt{3}$. The constant term is their product: $c=(-10+\sqrt{3})(-10-\sqrt{3})=100-3=97$. The answer is 97.
